title: SPIKE: Understand User-to-Team Mapping Data Model
description:
Investigate and clarify the relationship between `dtp_user.CURRENT_TEAM_ID` and the `dtp_team_user` mapping table. A scenario was identified where one user ID is tied to two different teams.

### Data Model
- **dtp_users** — user records
- **dtp_teams** — team records
- **dtp_team_user** — many-to-many mapping between users and teams

### Question
Can `dtp_user.CURRENT_TEAM_ID` be relied upon as the user's primary/current team when the mapping table shows them in multiple teams?

### Findings
The `dtp_team_user` table manages the many-to-many relationship. `CURRENT_TEAM_ID` on the user record indicates their active team context, while the mapping table tracks all team memberships.
data_model_changes: Clarified the role of dtp_user.CURRENT_TEAM_ID as the active team context vs. dtp_team_user for full team membership history.
implementation_notes: This was a spike/investigation ticket. The three-table structure (dtp_users, dtp_teams, dtp_team_user) uses a standard many-to-many pattern. CURRENT_TEAM_ID on the user table serves as the active team selector.
comment_summary: Developer explained the many-to-many mapping table structure and confirmed the three-table relationship. The spike concluded without code changes.
